nodes.name: Alphabets
nodes.description: Phonetic alphabets, classical alphabets, and letter systems

config.prompt: Name all 24 letters of the Greek alphabet in order

items.explanation:
1st letter; uppercase Α, lowercase α
From Phoenician aleph ('ox'); the Greek letter name has no meaning in Greek itself
In science, α denotes the first in a series: alpha particle, alpha radiation, alpha male
The phrase 'alpha and omega' (first and last) appears in the Book of Revelation to describe God

2nd letter; uppercase Β, lowercase β
From Phoenician beth ('house'); alpha + beta = 'alphabet'
In software, 'beta' denotes a pre-release testing phase, after 'alpha'
In finance, beta (β) measures a stock's volatility relative to the overall market

3rd letter; uppercase Γ, lowercase γ
Gamma rays are the highest-energy form of electromagnetic radiation, discovered by Paul Villard in 1900
In photography and display technology, gamma correction adjusts brightness curves
The Gamma function (Γ) in mathematics generalizes the factorial to non-integer values

4th letter; uppercase Δ, lowercase δ
The uppercase Δ is a triangle, giving its name to river deltas (Herodotus named the Nile Delta for its triangular shape)
In math and science, Δ means 'change in' a quantity (Δx, ΔT)
Also the NATO phonetic word for the letter D

5th letter; uppercase Ε, lowercase ε
In calculus and analysis, ε represents an arbitrarily small positive quantity ('for every ε > 0')
The ε–δ definition of a limit, formalized by Weierstrass, is foundational to modern analysis
The Latin letter E derives directly from epsilon

6th letter; uppercase Ζ, lowercase ζ
The Riemann zeta function ζ(s) is central to number theory; the Riemann Hypothesis about its zeros is one of math's greatest unsolved problems
Catherine Zeta-Jones's middle name comes from her grandmother
Zeta is used in fluid dynamics for vorticity

7th letter; uppercase Η, lowercase η
In physics, η commonly represents efficiency (ratio of useful output to total input)
'ETA' as an abbreviation for 'estimated time of arrival' is unrelated to the Greek letter
The Basque separatist group ETA (Euskadi Ta Askatasuna) is also unrelated

8th letter; uppercase Θ, lowercase θ
The standard symbol for an angle in trigonometry and geometry
In ancient Athens, jurors used Θ (for thanatos, 'death') to vote for a death sentence
Theta waves (4–8 Hz) in neuroscience are associated with drowsiness and light sleep

9th letter; uppercase Ι, lowercase ι
The smallest letter in the Greek alphabet, giving English the phrase 'not one iota' (meaning 'not the slightest bit')
From Jesus's words in Matthew 5:18: 'not one jot or tittle' (jot = iota)
The Latin letter I and the English letter J both derive from iota

10th letter; uppercase Κ, lowercase κ
In statistics, Cohen's kappa (κ) measures inter-rater agreement beyond chance
Kappa Kappa Gamma, founded 1870 at Monmouth College, is one of the oldest women's fraternities
In internet culture, 'Kappa' is a Twitch emote indicating sarcasm

11th letter; uppercase Λ, lowercase λ
In physics, λ represents wavelength; in linear algebra, eigenvalues
The Spartan army used Λ on their shields, standing for Lacedaemon (Sparta's official name)
AWS Lambda (2014) popularized 'serverless computing'; the name references lambda calculus

12th letter; uppercase Μ, lowercase μ
The SI prefix 'micro-' uses μ (one millionth, 10⁻⁶)
In statistics, μ represents the population mean
In physics, μ is the coefficient of friction and also denotes the muon (elementary particle)

13th letter; uppercase Ν, lowercase ν
In physics, ν (nu) represents frequency of a wave
Easily confused with the Latin letter 'v' in handwriting — a frequent source of errors in physics notation
From Phoenician nun ('fish'); the Latin N derives from this letter

14th letter; uppercase Ξ, lowercase ξ
One of the most difficult Greek letters for English speakers to write and pronounce (English: /zaɪ/ or /ksaɪ/)
Used in particle physics for the Xi baryon (Ξ)
The uppercase Ξ resembles a stack of three horizontal lines

15th letter; uppercase Ο, lowercase ο
Means 'small O' (o mikron) as opposed to omega ('big O')
Became globally known as the name of a COVID-19 variant designated by the WHO in November 2021
Rarely used as a scientific symbol because it is visually identical to the Latin letter O

16th letter; uppercase Π, lowercase π
The most famous Greek letter in mathematics: π ≈ 3.14159, the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter
First used for this constant by Welsh mathematician William Jones in 1706, popularized by Euler
Pi Day is celebrated on March 14 (3/14); in 2015, the date 3/14/15 matched the first five digits

17th letter; uppercase Ρ, lowercase ρ
In physics, ρ represents density; in statistics, it denotes the correlation coefficient
The Chi-Rho (☧) monogram, combining the first two letters of 'Christ' in Greek (ΧΡΙΣΤΟΣ), was an early Christian symbol adopted by Emperor Constantine
Visually similar to the Latin letter P but represents the 'r' sound

18th letter; uppercase Σ, lowercase σ (or ς at end of a word)
In math, Σ is the summation symbol; in statistics, σ is standard deviation
'Six Sigma' is a quality management methodology aiming for fewer than 3.4 defects per million opportunities, developed at Motorola in 1986
The word-final form ς (final sigma) is one of the few positional letter variants in Greek

19th letter; uppercase Τ, lowercase τ
Some mathematicians advocate τ = 2π as a more natural circle constant (one full turn rather than half)
In physics, τ represents torque, shear stress, and the tau lepton (a heavy electron discovered in 1975)
The Tau cross (T-shape) is associated with Saint Anthony and is also called a Saint Anthony's cross

20th letter; uppercase Υ, lowercase υ
The Latin letters U, V, W, and Y all ultimately derive from upsilon
In particle physics, the upsilon meson (Υ) was discovered at Fermilab in 1977
The name means 'simple u' (u psilon), distinguishing it from the diphthong oi which had come to sound the same

21st letter; uppercase Φ, lowercase φ
The golden ratio (1.618...) is often denoted by φ (phi), named after the sculptor Phidias
In engineering, φ represents phase angle; in statistics, the phi coefficient measures association
Phi Beta Kappa, founded at the College of William & Mary in 1776, is the oldest academic honor society in the U.S.

22nd letter; uppercase Χ, lowercase χ
The Chi-squared (χ²) test is one of the most widely used statistical tests
The Chi-Rho symbol (☧) combines chi and rho, the first two letters of Christ (Χριστός) in Greek
Often confused with the Latin X; 'Xmas' uses the Greek chi (Χ) as an abbreviation for 'Christ'

23rd letter; uppercase Ψ, lowercase ψ
In quantum mechanics, ψ is the wave function describing a particle's quantum state (Schrödinger's equation)
The trident-shaped uppercase Ψ is used as the symbol for psychology (from psyche, 'soul/mind')
In parapsychology, 'psi' is a blanket term for psychic phenomena

24th and final letter; uppercase Ω, lowercase ω
Means 'great O' (o mega) as opposed to omicron ('small O')
In physics, Ω is the unit of electrical resistance (ohm), named after Georg Ohm
The phrase 'alpha and omega' means 'the beginning and the end,' from the Book of Revelation
Omega-3 fatty acids are named because the first double bond is at the 3rd carbon from the omega (terminal) end
